author: ijjk

body: Native TS is enabled from [v22.18.0](https://nodejs.org/en/blog/release/v22.18.0), and unit tests ran on v22.19.0, so `process.features.typescript` existed. Will get that added to the docs as well.
author: devjiwonchoi

body:
[vc codeowners]: #

Notifying the following users due to files changed in this PR based on this repo's [notify modifiers](https://vercel.com/docs/workflow-collaboration/code-owners/code-approvers#notify):

@timneutkens, @ijjk, @shuding, @huozhi:
```
packages/next/src/server/config.ts
```
<!-- Edited to fix team @ mention -->

body: ## [CodSpeed Performance Report](https://codspeed.io/vercel/next.js/branches/huozhi%2F09_08-metadata-routes-params-async) ### Merging #83560 will **not alter performance** Comparing huozhi/09_08-metadata-routes-params-async (dace792) with canary (c9fb1e5) ### Summary `✅ 9` untouched benchmarks
author: codspeed-hq

body:
Hey @huozhi I have a question regarding this change. I completely understand why its necessary, however, how can we statically optimize images at build time that depend on params the same way we would for dynamic page segments using something like `generateStaticParams`?

We can't `'use cache'` on a route handler, and although my dynamic OG images depend on the params, that is only to use the slug to fetch cached data to generate the image, but all of this can and should happen at build time. There is no runtime information needed.

Please correct me if I'm understanding this incorrectly, but I would like a `generateStaticParams` like solution for these OG image routes so that I do not need to use runtime compute to generate this images.
author: PlagueFPS

body:
why is that `initWebpack` call even needed?
shouldn't it be enough to just install the require aliases.
then webpack would simply execute only when loaded

https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/0ec20967aa720a69f18a40f39288bd8ca9012cf2/packages/next/src/server/config-utils.ts#L10
author: mischnic

body: Yeah I'm really not sure why. Need to dig into that

Please let me know if any further changes are needed.
